back_translation: A man from the household of Levi went and took a daughter of Levi as his wife.
The woman conceived and bore a son. When she saw that he was a good child, she hid him for three months.
When she could no longer hide him, she took for him a basket of papyrus, coated it with tar and pitch, placed the child in the basket, and set it among the reeds at the bank of the Nile.
His sister stood at a distance to see what would happen to him.
The daughter of Pharaoh went down to bathe at the Nile, while her maidservants walked along the riverbank. She saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maidservant to retrieve it.
When she opened it, she saw the child, and behold, the infant was crying. She had compassion on him and said, 'This is one of the Hebrew children.'
His sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, 'Shall I go and call a Hebrew nursing woman to nurse the child for you?'
Pharaoh's daughter said to her, 'Go.' So the young woman went and called the mother of the child.
Pharaoh's daughter said to her, 'Take this child and nurse him for me, and I will pay your wages.' So the woman took the child and nursed him.
When the child grew older, she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses, saying, 'Because I drew him up from the water.'
Many years later, when Moses had grown up, he went out to his brothers and saw their hard labor. He saw an Egyptian striking a Hebrew, one of his brothers.
He looked all around and saw no one; he struck down the Egyptian and hid the body in the sand.
The next day he went out again, and behold, two Hebrew men were fighting. He asked the one in the wrong, 'Why are you striking your companion?'
He replied, 'Who made you ruler and judge over us? Are you planning to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?' Moses was afraid and thought, 'Surely the matter has become known.'
When Pharaoh heard of this matter, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the presence of Pharaoh, went to live in the land of Midian, and sat down beside a well.
The priest of Midian had seven daughters; they came to draw water and fill the troughs to water their father's flock.
Shepherds came and drove them away, but Moses rose up to help them and watered their flock.
When they returned to Reuel their father, he asked, 'Why have you returned so quickly today?'
They said, 'An Egyptian rescued us from the hand of the shepherds; he also drew water for us and watered the flock.'
He asked his daughters, 'Where is he? Why did you leave the man? Invite him to eat food.'
Moses agreed to live with the man, and he gave Zipporah his daughter to Moses.
She bore a son, and Moses named him Gershom, saying, 'I have been a sojourner in a foreign land.'
After many long years, the king of Egypt died. The sons of Israel groaned and cried out because of the slavery, and their cry from the slavery ascended to God.
God heard their groaning, and God remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
God saw the sons of Israel, and God knew.
